nodes.label: AI as research consultant providing options
nodes.grounding_span: I don't like using AI to just do the work for me, instead I like to use it to give me the pieces and let me put them together

nodes.label: AI as semantic search and explanation tool
nodes.grounding_span: Having it as a semantic search instead of a literal search has been extremely useful

nodes.label: AI as machine-like tool with factual output
nodes.grounding_span: I don't want a person helping me the whole value is that it is a machine processing data

nodes.label: AI for uninteresting placeholder tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI for core creative or interesting tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Stuff that I could probably figure out, but isn't all that interesting to me

nodes.label: AI as tool for learning and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I hope that by using AI to learn about things I am passionate about, and not let it just do all the work for me

nodes.label: AI as creator of new data vs user of historical data
nodes.label_negative: AI as user of historical data
nodes.grounding_span: AI is really good at using historical data, so I want to be someone who is creating new data instead of using old data

nodes.label: Personal accomplishment and satisfaction
nodes.grounding_span: I want to feel the sense of accomplishing a task

nodes.label: Understanding and maintainability
nodes.grounding_span: if I don't understand how the code is working ... then it becomes much hard to refer back to it

nodes.label: Efficiency and time saving
nodes.grounding_span: the time to relearn it and debug it is just not something I want to do

nodes.label: Reliable factual output
nodes.grounding_span: I don't want a person helping me the whole value is that it is a machine processing data

nodes.label: Innovation and creating new knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: I want to be someone who is creating new data instead of using old data

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as semantic search
nodes.grounding_span: Having it as a semantic search instead of a literal search has been extremely useful

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's human-like overconfidence
nodes.grounding_span: If it makes a mistake then it says 'Oops' ... I don't want a person helping me

nodes.label: Preference for AI as consultant not doer
nodes.grounding_span: I don't like using AI to just do the work for me, instead I like to use it to give me the pieces

nodes.label: Hope that AI helps not replaces
nodes.grounding_span: I can only hope its here to help and not replace

nodes.label: Modular task decomposition — breaks tasks into small, testable components
nodes.grounding_span: I just give it what the input is, and what I need the output to be, and let it handle it. Test it to make sure its working

nodes.label: Contextual boilerplate approach — provides fresh context per query to avoid history issues
nodes.grounding_span: I find it easier to just have a boilerplate copy paste of the context it needs, then give it a question with that context
